content.question: Can a phased, piloted reform sequence be credibly shielded from premature politicization and stakeholder anxiety without a pre-commitment mechanism (e.g., cross-party agreement, multi-year autonomy for pilot schools, or constitutional protection)?
content.reduction_path: Convene a structured consensus-building process with representatives from Fidesz, Tisza, teacher unions, and parent organizations to establish explicit guardrails (protected pilot period, agreed success metrics, staged timeline) before any pilot launch; document agreement in formal MOUs; monitor political attack frequency and intensity during pilot phase to establish whether such pre-commitment reduces cycle-time volatility; compare pilot-phase stability to the June 2026 ministerial-statement baseline (24-48 hour politicization).
